content.content: The overall peace and security situation of the district thus far was analyzed and found to be satisfactory.

Approved various security and action plans for F.Y. 2082/083, including: VVIP security, security for potential programs by the Provincial Renaming Joint Struggle Committee, Holi festival security, general festival security, security for important locations, action plans for controlling improper resource extraction, security for fairs/festivals, and action plans for controlling religious discrimination and untouchability.
Approved the 'QRT/IRT Deployment 2082' plan, including available equipment, to cover all local levels of Dhankuta district.

In view of the currently increasing security challenges in the country, all security agencies shall make their information mechanisms more effective, disseminate factual information promptly, and remain in a state of readiness for immediate deployment as needed to ensure reliable security.

Analyzing the situation following Gen Z protests in Kathmandu Valley and other locations on 2082/05/23, it is decided to issue a curfew order under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. This applies to the following locations in Dhankuta Municipality, Dhankuta district, from 5:00 PM (17:00) on August 25, 2082, until 6:00 AM on August 26, 2082, or until further notice.
North: Kagate
East: Khaniya Bas
West: Industrial Village
South: Chuliban
The overall peace and security situation of the district thus far was analyzed and found to be satisfactory.

Following the Gen Z protests and the resulting situation, a Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) is issued for the entire Dhankuta district under Section 6(3a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, effective from 11:00 AM on 2082/05/26 until further notice. Assembly, processions, demonstrations, meetings, or picketing are prohibited.

To prevent potential incidents, a curfew order is issued under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, for the following locations in Dhankuta Municipality from 8:00 PM (20:00) on August 26, 2082, until 4:00 AM on August 27, 2082.
North: Kagate
East: Khaniya Bas
West: Industrial Village
South: Chuliban
The current peace and security situation of the district was felt to be sensitive.

While the movement on 2082/05/23 was peaceful, the Gen Z protests and slogans starting the afternoon of 2082/05/24 have become unruly. Vandalism has occurred in government offices, and attacks, vandalism, and arson are being reported at political party offices and residences of people's representatives. Activities disrupting peace have simultaneously started in the district headquarters, other municipalities, and urban areas. As the situation cannot be controlled solely by local Nepal Police and APF units, it is decided to request immediate assistance from the Nepalese Army (Pashupati Prasad Battalion) to prevent further damage.
Decided to deploy Nepal Police and Armed Police Force across the district under the leadership/coordination of the Pashupati Prasad Battalion.
Decided to issue orders for restricted zones, prohibitory orders, and curfews in the district as necessary.
Decided to bring the situation under control using minimum force during security operations.

Evaluated the damage from the Gen Z protests on 2082/05/24, noting vandalism of government offices, arson at the municipality and party offices, and attacks on the homes of current and former representatives, resulting in significant loss of property, physical structures, and important documents.
Based on the security assessment, it is deemed necessary to impose a curfew in the following areas. A recommendation is made to the Chief District Officer to issue the curfew order.
District Headquarters, Triyuga Municipality Area:
East: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 13, Phuljor
West: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 7, Chuhade
North: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 10, Chapan Baruwa Bridge
South: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 6, Jaljale
Katari Municipality Area:
East: Katari Municipality Ward No. 5, Dangdung Khola Bridge
West: Katari Municipality Ward No. 4, Kakur Khola Bridge
North: Katari Municipality Ward No. 4, Maruwa Khola Bridge
South: Katari Municipality Ward No. 3, Taradhari
Chaudandigadhi Municipality Area:
East: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 4, Basaha
West: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 6, Satdhare
North: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 8, Birendra Bazar
South: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 6, Madan Bhandari Highway
Decided to appeal to political parties, civil society, intellectuals, journalists, and all stakeholders to help maintain peace and order in the district.
Decided to mobilize District Police and APF Nepal No. 5 Battalion HQ (Triyuga, Udayapur) under the leadership of the Nepalese Army Pashupati Prasad Battalion to maintain security.
Decided to manage security through the District Security Command Post and JOC based on a detailed assessment of the overall security situation.

Evaluating the security situation following the Gen Z protests on 2082/05/24, it is decided to recommend that the Chief District Officer issue a district-wide Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) effective from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM on 2082/05/26.
To maintain order, it is decided to recommend the re-issuance of the curfew order for the following areas from 7:00 PM tonight (2082/05/26) until 5:00 AM tomorrow morning.
East: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 13, Phuljor
West: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 7, Chuhade
North: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 10, Chapan Baruwa Bridge
South: Triyuga Municipality Ward No. 6, Jaljale
East: Katari Municipality Ward No. 5, Dangdung Khola Bridge
West: Katari Municipality Ward No. 4, Kakur Khola Bridge
North: Katari Municipality Ward No. 4, Maruwa Khola Bridge
South: Katari Municipality Ward No. 3, Taradhari
East: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 4, Basaha
West: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 6, Satdhare
North: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 8, Birendra Bazar
South: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 6, Madan Bhandari Highway

Upon evaluating the peace and security situation within the district, it has been decided to recommend that the Chief District Officer issue a curfew order for the following additional areas, effective from 07:00 PM tonight until 05:00 AM tomorrow morning.
East: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 9, Kharkhola
West: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 4, Lalbazar
North: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 9, Bhachuwa Water Tank
South: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 3, Gideri Khola
Decided to request all political parties, civil society, intellectuals, journalists, and stakeholders to help maintain peace, security, and order in the district.
Decided to mobilize the Nepalese Army, Nepal Police, and Armed Police Force to maintain security arrangements in the district.
Decided to manage security through the District Security Command Post and the JOC based on a micro-evaluation of the overall security situation.

Discussions were held regarding potential security challenges arising from 'Gen-Z' protests currently taking place in Kathmandu and most districts against the government regarding the social media ban, corruption, and other issues. Given the possibility of similar protests in this district, security agencies shall coordinate to develop a security plan and ensure reliable arrangements to keep demonstrations peaceful and prevent any untoward incidents.
In view of the upcoming major festivals such as Dashain, Tihar, and Chhath, all three security agencies shall prepare and effectively implement an integrated festival-centric security action plan.
The District Police Office and the APF Dependent Gulm shall increase regular daily patrolling from all their respective units within the district.

A strategic sectoral security plan, in accordance with the Integrated District Security Plan prepared by the District Security Committee for the current fiscal year, shall be prepared in coordination with all security agencies and presented at the next meeting.
The meeting discussed the nationwide Gen Z protests, their impact on Okhaldhunga district, and future security strategies. The security situation thus far has been found to be normal.

Expressed heartfelt tributes to those who lost their lives during protests in Kathmandu and other parts of the country since yesterday, extended deep condolences to the bereaved families, and wished for the speedy recovery of the injured.

Information has been received that protests may occur in this district in solidarity with the Gen Z protests that began yesterday (2082/05/23). Security agencies shall prioritize the safety of citizens' lives and property, with special focus on securing public assets (government offices, vehicles, local levels, residences of high-ranking officials, sensitive physical structures, and petrol pumps).

To normalize the impact of Gen Z protests and prevent untoward incidents, coordination shall be maintained with district political party leaders/representatives, civil society, local intellectuals, distinguished personalities, and agitating parties. Security agencies shall exercise full patience.
A sincere appeal is made to all district residents to keep demonstrations peaceful and disciplined, prevent self-interested infiltration, and protect public property.

content: A curfew order is issued under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, effective from 5:00 PM on 2082/05/25 until 6:00 AM on 2082/05/26. Movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, meetings, or picketing are prohibited within the following areas of Okhaldhunga district.

content.content: East: All areas of Rumjatar, Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 4 and 5
West: All areas of Rampur (Molung Rural Municipality Ward No. 1 and 2) and Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 9, 11, and 12
North: All areas of Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 6 and 7
South: All areas of Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 3, 8, and 10

Analyzing the critical situation arising from the protests on 2082/05/23, including arson, vandalism, and looting of property, as well as incidents of prisoners escaping from various district jails, there is a high probability of similar incidents in this district. As it may be difficult for the Nepal Police and Armed Police Force alone to control the situation and prevent criminal acts like theft and looting, it is decided to request additional assistance from the Nepalese Army (Durgabax Battalion) under Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.
The peace and security situation of the district thus far was found to be normal.
